description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2337 (MacBook Air 13" M1). Follow sequentially from Step 1. USB-C only (no MagSafe). Two USB-C ports (ATC0 left rear, ATC1 left front).
WARNING: A2337 (820-02016) M1 Air ONLY. PMU naming: SERA=Master (U8100), SIMETRA=Slave (U7700). SVR AON is 30A design (NOT 42A). No MagSafe, no Viper VR, no Monaco VR. Simpler topology than Pro/Max.
data_source: Schematic PNGs (pages 22-23, 25, 28, 32, 37-38, 55) cross-referenced with saf-diagnostics-2026-03-02.json (15 A2337 logic board cases, 22 total devices). Power-on sequence verified against Eason (iphone_cpu_fix) M1 standby circuit reference PDF.

eason_reference.source: M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit (Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix)
power_on_sequence: 1. PPVBUS_USBC0 5V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPHV_INT0_AONSW → FF200 → PPDCIN_AONSW
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → R5220 → PPDCIN_AON_CHGR_R → U5200 (power). R5215/R5216 → CHGR_AUX_DET → U5200 (detect)
3. U5200 → Q5230/Q5240 → PPBUS_AON (12.6V temp). CHGR_EN_MVR → R5340/U5340 → P3V8AON_PWR_EN → R5710 → U5700
5. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → U8100(MPMU) + U8110 comparator. R8100/R8101 → MPMU_SNS_DIV → U8110. When same>opposite, PMU_VDD_HI(3.8V) → U8100 voltage detect
6. U8100 → all MPMU rails + PP3V3_S2_UPC/PP1V8_S2 to CD3217. UC100→PP3V3_AON, UC120→PP1V8_AON. U7700 → SPMU rails
7. M1 hard start (Y0600 24MHz + Y8301 32.768KHz + PMU_RESET_L). M1 reads ROM, I2C to CD3217
8. M1 → U8100 → P5VS2_PWR_EN → UC300 → PP5V_S2 (AFTER hard start, not during)
9. CD3217 reads shared ROM (4 SPI lines), sends PD via CC1/CC2 → charger boosts to 20V
10. 20V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPDCIN_AONSW → U5200 → real PPBUS_AON (12.6V)

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation to test if fault is pre-SVR or post-SVR. Critical diagnostic shortcut from Eason reference.
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (use 4.3V if testing with display attached)
2. Jumper U8110 pins 4 and 5 to force PMU_VDD_HI high
3. Press start button OR connect to charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: Fault is in the FRONT END — protection isolation and 5V→20V boost circuit (CD3217, charger IC, fuses). Check Steps 1-2 of diagnostic tree.
interpretation.forced_start_fails: Fault is in the BACK END — M1 working conditions (PMU, SoC power, clocks, ROM). Check Steps 3+ of diagnostic tree.

ppvaon_fault_l.description: Active-low fault flag from SVR AON controller U5700. If LOW, SVR has detected overcurrent/overvoltage/thermal fault — the SVR itself has a problem. If HIGH, SVR is healthy, look downstream.
ppvaon_fault_l.action: Single probe eliminates entire SVR AON section

pmu_active_ready.description: Goes HIGH only after MPMU (U8100 SERA) completes full startup sequence. If PP3V8_AON present but PMU_ACTIVE_READY stays low, fault is inside PMU, clocks, ROM, or SoC handshake.
pmu_active_ready.action: Skips past power section to compute section

crystal_32khz.description: PMU_REFCLK32K_OUT — MPMU cannot start without this clock. No oscillation = dead crystal = board won't start regardless of power path health.
crystal_32khz.action: 1 second with oscilloscope eliminates entire clock failure category

soc_force_dfu.description: Forces DFU mode bypassing macOS boot. If DFU works but board won't boot normally, entire power delivery and SoC hardware is fine — problem is NAND/firmware.
soc_force_dfu.action: Instantly separates hardware from software faults. No disassembly needed.

phase_balance.description: Individual phase current sense resistors for the 3-phase SVR AON. Measure voltage drop across each while running. Zero on one while others are equal = dead phase (open FET, open inductor, bad driver).
phase_balance.action: Identifies exact dead phase without removing anything

cd3217_sys_detect.description: Goes high when charger IC recognizes valid power source. 5V at USB-C but SYS_DETECT low = CD3217 fault. No 5V at port = cable/connector/port controller.
cd3217_sys_detect.action: Splits entire input path in one probe

pp5v_s2_bootstrap.description: PP5V_S2 feeds back to PP3V8_AON VDRV after hard start to sustain SVR AON driver voltage. If board starts then dies after ~1 second, hard start works but bootstrap path is broken.

tree.probe: Bulk capacitor near U5200 charger IC output (page 23). CZ243/CZ245 area.

no.saf_context: SAF: 3 cases at 5V with no amps — CD3217 failure and charging port repair were root causes. 1 case (BM 1170) at 5V: 'repair logic board one CD chips and charging port'. 1 case (BM 946): 'repair headphone jack logic board flex and charging port'.
check_sequence: Check PPVBUS_USBC0/USBC1 at USB-C connector pins (page 57) — should have 5V or 20V from adapter
Check CD3217 port controllers: UF400 (ATC0, page 55) and UF500 (ATC1, page 56) — SAF: CD3217 failure is #1 cause of no-power on A2337
Check SPI ROM XF420 (page 55) — if corrupted, CD3217 won't negotiate PD
Check SYS_DETECT from battery connector J5100/Q5150 (page 22) — charger needs battery detect
Check charger IC U5200 gate drives CHGR_GATE_G1-G7 (page 23)
Check dual inductors L5230 (page 23)
Check F5200 fuse — if blown, no input to charger
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs (Q5240/Q5243/Q5244)

tree.probe: PP3V8_AON output caps near U5700 (page 25). B5700/CS751/CS752 area.

no.saf_context: SAF: BM 1111 at 20V/0.5A (low amp) — battery + keyboard replacement suggests partial boot stalling at SVR/PMU level. BM 1067 at 20V/1.4A with liquid damage — liquid on PP3V8_AON distribution is common.
check_sequence: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN (derived from SYS_DETECT via D5340/D5345 delay, page 25)
Check PPVAON_FAULT_L (active low fault signal, page 25) — if low, U5700 reports fault
Diode mode on PP3V8_AON output caps — check for short on VDDMAIN branch
Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN and PP3V8_AON_VODMAN branches separately (page 25, aliases page 79)
Check 3-phase driver FETs: Q5800 (DRVL1 via L5800), Q5820 (DRVL2 via L5820), Q5840 (DRVL3 via L5840) — each through sense resistors to PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN (per Eason reference)
Check U5910 current sense amplifier
Thermal cam on U5700 and phase FETs Q5800/Q5820/Q5840 at low voltage (5V current-limited)
SHORTCUT: Try Eason forced start bypass — inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN + jumper U8110 pins 4+5 + press start. If board boots, fault is upstream of SVR.

tree.probe: UC100 output CC101 for PP3V3_AON, UC120 output CC121 for PP1V8_AON (page 36)

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_VODMAN input to LDOs — if missing, fault is upstream at U5700
If PP3V3_AON missing: check UC100 input/output, diode mode on output cap CC101
If PP1V8_AON missing: check UC120 input/output, diode mode on output cap CC121
Thermal cam on LDO area for hot component

tree.probe: Slave PMU buck output caps (page 28). BUCK4 through BUCK14 output capacitor banks.
tree.expect: Multiple buck rails present — check SIMETRA_BUCK4/5/6/10/12/13/14 outputs

no.saf_context: SAF: BM 1067 at 20V/1.4A — liquid damage on logic board required cleaning. Liquid often corrodes PMU BGA pads on M1 boards.
check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN input to U7700 (page 28)
Check SIMETRA buck enable signals (pages 28-31)
Check for cap short on individual buck outputs — use diode mode across output cap banks C77xx
Check SIMETRA_ACTIVE_READY signal
Thermal cam on U7700 area — if PMU is hot, internal short or downstream loading
If single buck missing: find downstream short on that rail before replacing PMU

tree.probe: Master PMU buck output caps (page 32). BUCK0 through BUCK11 output capacitor banks. Also check DB110/QR110 power button circuit.
tree.expect: Multiple buck rails present — check SERA_BUCK0/1/2/3/7/8/9/11 outputs

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN input to U8100 (page 32)

CRITICAL (Eason ref): Check U8110 comparator — PPBUS_AON via R8100/R8101 → MPMU_SNS_DIV → U8110 same-direction input. PP3V8_AON feeds U8110 opposite input. If same > opposite, U8110 outputs PMU_VDD_HI (3.8V) to U8100 as voltage detection. Without PMU_VDD_HI, U8100 will NOT start.
Check Y8301 crystal (32.768KHz) — U8100 needs this clock to start (per Eason reference)
Check PMU_ONOFF_L — pulled up by PP1V8_AON_MPMU through R8324
Check SERA buck enable signals (pages 32-35)
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY output
Check power button circuit: DB110 controller, QR110 (page 32)
Check for cap short on individual buck outputs — diode mode across output cap banks C81xx/C82xx
Thermal cam on U8100 area
If LDO outputs missing (pages 34-35): check SERA_LDO1/2/3/7/9/10/13/16

tree.probe: UC300 output (page 38) — PP5V_S2 probe points PPC10-PPC15 (page 37). UC710 output (page 39) — PP3V3_S2 probe points PPC210-PPC215 (page 37).

no.diagnosis: Standalone VR enable or IC fault
check_sequence: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal (page 37) — gated by PMU GPIO
Check P3V3S2_PWR_EN gating logic (page 37) — NOTE: Per Sera PMU OFF, GPIO11 is OFF when System is OFF
If PP5V_S2 missing: check UC300 (SMC322) input PPBUS_AON, output inductor LC320, sense XMC320
If PP3V3_S2 missing: check UC710 input, output inductor LC710, sense XMC710/XMC711
Check PP3V3_S2 and PP5V_S2 discharge circuits (page 37) — JC211/JC220 jumpers
Diode mode on output caps for shorts downstream

tree.probe: SoC power pages 11-15. PCPU from SERA BUCK0, ECPU from SERA BUCK11, GPU from SIMETRA BUCK6, DDR from SERA BUCK2/SIMETRA BUCK5.
tree.expect: Multiple voltage domains present at SoC pins. Board should attempt boot.

no.diagnosis: SoC fault or SPI ROM fault
no.saf_context: SAF: BM 1313 at 20V/2.5A (5V column) — logic board repair + audio IC. Normal boot current but SoC-adjacent faults. BM 1223: 'repair logic board sound ic'. Audio IC is near SoC power domains on A2337.
check_sequence: Check SPI NOR flash (page 19) — if SoC can't read firmware, no boot
Check secure element (page 21)
Check SoC ground connections (pages 16-17) — open ground can cause no-boot
Check NAND/storage power (pages 62-65) — SoC needs storage for boot
If all power present but no Apple logo: try DFU mode via USB-C

substeps.probe: DP600 (BEN) backlight boost (page 69), display connector (page 67), UP700/UP705/UP715 power sequencer (page 68)
substeps.note: A2337 uses DP600 for backlight (NOT U6560 as on A2681)

substeps.probe: UF400 (ATC0 left rear, page 55), UF500 (ATC1 left front, page 56), connectors (page 57)
substeps.note: No MagSafe on A2337 — USB-C is the ONLY power/data path. Both ports are on the left side.
substeps.saf_context: SAF: CD3217 (UF400/UF500) is the most common IC failure on A2337 after keyboard
SAF: 9 of 15 logic board cases involved keyboard repair — highest single fault on A2337. Often paired with keyboard backlight (A2179/A2337 shared part).
SAF: BM 1223 — 'repair audio logic board' and 'audio logic board repair' at 5V. BM 1313 — 'logic board and audio logic board repair' at 20V/2.5A. Audio IC failures happen on A2337.

substeps.note: A2337 uses Rasputin (NOT Typhoon as on A2681)
M1 NAND is paired to SoC. If NAND fault confirmed, board may be unrepairable.

substeps.saf_context: SAF: BM 1366 — liquid damage caused trackpad failure alongside charging port and keyboard. Trackpad flex A2179/A2337 shared part.
